health_zone_table.basis: SitRep51 Table 2 (04 July 2026) visually reviewed against the INSP PDF (SitRep_MVE_RDC_N°051_04_07_2026 sha256 b01c0699fb60a6be23703c91b657994b3bdc25dc200f98f561bae3177fedc767, 12 pages), byte-verified against the live INSP upload. Report date is 04 July 2026 (publication 05 July 2026).

reconciliation.note: Rows promoted here reconcile to official province/national totals: 1544 named confirmed cases plus 17 Ituri cases awaiting ventilation = 1561; all 506 confirmed deaths are named-zone attributed. The residual row is retained as unventilated context and excluded from mappable named-zone attribution.

lab_indicators_24h.note: National aggregate from SitRep51 section 4.3: Ituri 99 collected/analyzed / 30 positive (30.3%); Nord-Kivu 73 collected/analyzed / 3 positive (4.1%); Sud-Kivu 2 collected and still under analysis (excluded from positivity). Positivity uses 33 positives over 172 analyzed samples.

care_continuity: SitRep51 re-reports the aggregate isolation census at 628 patients (89.4% occupancy) in the banner, unchanged from SitRep50, and again published NO fresh patient-movement table this cycle. The confirmed/suspected split (215 confirmed / 413 suspected) is therefore carried from the last published SitRep49 split, which still reconciles to the 628 census (215 + 413 = 628); it is a carried/reconciled estimate, not a freshly reported table. Nord-Kivu isolation runs at high occupancy (challenge table cites 138.8% saturation).

Continuity of care reports 109 confirmed people-deprived-of-liberty (PPL) cumulative in Ituri, of whom 34 died (31.2%) and 46 recovered. 778 hot meals were served (138 to confirmed cases, 133 to suspected cases, 454 to PPLs, 53 to caregivers).
highlights: 33 new confirmed cases including 6 community deaths were reported for 2026-07-04, in Ituri (30 including 6 deaths) and Nord-Kivu (3). In total 14 confirmed deaths were recorded over the day: the 6 community deaths plus 8 deaths among previously confirmed CTE patients (7 Ituri, 1 Nord-Kivu Katwa), bringing confirmed deaths to 506.
The 33 new cases came from Rwampara (11), Bunia (7), Nizi (7), Komanda (3) and Mongbwalu (2) in Ituri and from Musienene (2) and Katwa (1) in Nord-Kivu.
No new affected health zone was reported on 2026-07-04; affected zones remain 36/104 and Ituri stays at 24/36 zones touched. Sud-Kivu has recorded no confirmed case since 2026-05-26.

Clinical trials began at the CTE CME in the Rwampara health zone (Ituri) this cycle; the Nord-Kivu response opened the CTE Beni (20-bed capacity) and transferred a confirmed PPL case from CT Musienene to CTE Katwa. Unlike SitRep50, no within-province reclassification was applied this cycle: Ituri is a clean +30 and Nord-Kivu a clean +3.

15 patients recovered (14 across Ituri CTEs Rwampara/Mongbwalu/Bunia/Komanda/CME/Elikya/Nyankunde, 1 Nord-Kivu Katwa), bringing DRC cumulative recoveries to 254. Mongbwalu carries a 50.7% case-fatality (141 deaths for 278 cases); Nord-Kivu remains the highest-lethality province at 57.4%, led by Katwa (73.1%), Oicha (66.7%) and Beni (58.6%).
laboratory: SitRep51 reports Ituri collected and analyzed 99 samples with 30 positive (30.3%).

Nord-Kivu collected and analyzed 73 samples with 3 positive (4.1%). Sud-Kivu collected 2 samples still under analysis. New diagnostic labs are being decentralized across the Ituri corridor (Komanda, Nizi, Mambasa, Nia-Nia).
publication_context: INSP SitRep 51 reports through 2026-07-04 and was published on the INSP WordPress site (post 25094; embedded PDF media 25095; WP publish 2026-07-05T20:26:47Z). The report date is 04 July 2026 (publication 05 July 2026).
SitRep51 supersedes SitRep50 directly (a single report-day advance); the cumulative rose by 33 confirmed and 14 deaths over 2026-07-04.
The outbreak is reported across 3 provinces and 36 of 104 health zones; no newly affected zone was reported this SitRep.
Ituri remains the epicenter with 1417 of 1561 DRC confirmed cases (90.8%) and 424 deaths (CFR 29.9%).
Nord-Kivu holds the highest provincial confirmed-case fatality ratio at 57.4%, led by Katwa (73.1%), Oicha (66.7%) and Beni (58.6%); 100% lethality observed in low-count Vuhovi.
redactions: Page contact details and operational names, if present outside table facts, are intentionally not promoted into public evidence text.
surveillance: 1244 alerts of the day were reported (15 J-1 + 1229 new) and 1002 investigated (Tableau 3), an 80.5% investigation rate; Ituri 52.7% (222/421), Nord-Kivu 100.0% (778/778), Sud-Kivu 4.4% (2/45).
354 suspected cases of the day were identified, including 110 deceased validated alerts.

10079 contacts were under follow-up and 8221 were seen, producing 81.6% follow-up coverage; Ituri 82.3%, Nord-Kivu 79.4%. The rate is computed only for reporting ZS (completeness 90%); Bunia, Aungba and Nia-Nia (Ituri) had no contact data at consolidation.

No consolidated daily new-contacts / 21-day-completed aggregate was published this cycle; new-contact listing is reported only at the sub-activity level (for example 27 persons listed around 2 positive cases at Shari and Tchunga, and 62 contacts pre-listed at CTE Rwampara, all in Ituri).
At PoE/PoC, 2 dead bodies were intercepted (both in Ituri). An IPC/EDS team was attacked and its vehicle stoned during decontamination at CMC Kiharo (Rughenda, Nord-Kivu).

Community-death analysis (SitRep51): in the hospital-lethality analysis cohort 94.2% of recorded deaths are community deaths (92.3%), concentrated in Mongbwalu (n=105 deaths), Bunia and Katwa, with late presentation to health structures raising the risk of secondary household transmission. These sub-cohort figures are qualitative context and are not used as denominators for the 506 confirmed-death total.

alerts_total.data_gap_note: Report (J-1) 15; new alerts 1229; total alerts of the day 1244. Tableau 3 investigated sum = 1002 (80.5%). No consolidated new-contacts / 21-day aggregate was published this cycle.

challenges.finding: Community reluctance toward post-mortem sampling (EDS/swabs) and resistance delay confirmation, underestimate cases, and disrupt key activities.
challenges.response: Intensify RCCE, involve religious and community leaders, and reinforce team security.

challenges.finding: Insufficient CTE capacity with Nord-Kivu saturation cited at 138.8% in the challenge table; the banner reports national isolation occupancy 89.4% (628 patients), raising nosocomial-transmission risk.
challenges.response: Accelerate CTE construction/expansion and deploy additional temporary isolation structures.

challenges.finding: Contact follow-up performance reached 81.6% (challenge table cites 81.3%) versus the 95% target, leaving transmission chains uninterrupted.
challenges.response: Train, deploy, and motivate community relays (RECO) with reinforced supervision.

challenges.finding: Weak alert reporting in some affected health zones risks undetected, silent transmission.
challenges.response: Reinforce community-based surveillance and rapid alert investigation.

challenges.finding: Seventeen Ituri confirmed cases remain not yet ventilated by health zone, leaving gaps in transmission-chain reconstruction.
challenges.response: Conduct data reconciliation, prune duplicates, and reclassify/localize with active field verification.

challenges.finding: Insufficient supply of essential medicines (MEG) and medical inputs weakens holistic case management.
challenges.response: Advocacy and urgent procurement of essential medicines.

challenges.finding: Insufficient IPC inputs (PPE, chlorine) and limited training raise nosocomial-infection risk; the challenge table reports 96 health-worker infections and 19 deaths (CFR 19.7%).
challenges.response: Urgent procurement and reinforced IPC training.

challenges.finding: A backlog of un-analyzed samples in Nord-Kivu and late diagnosis persist amid a 54.2% provincial CFR (challenge table).
challenges.response: Deploy a laboratory catch-up plan and reinforce diagnostic capacity.

challenges.finding: Insufficient ambulances and isolation structures (a gap of about 20 centers) delay transfer and isolation and raise transmission risk.
challenges.response: Urgent deployment of logistics and additional infrastructure.

challenges.finding: Insufficient coordination and weak use of COUSP frameworks cause operational inefficiency and delayed decisions.
challenges.response: Reinforce coordination mechanisms at all levels and operationalize provincial COUSP.

challenges.finding: A financial-resource gap of about USD 20 million limits implementation across all response pillars.
challenges.response: Urgent resource mobilization and partner advocacy.

challenges.finding: Insecurity and limited access (CODECO, ADF) restrict operations and access to high-risk zones.
challenges.response: Strengthen security coordination and humanitarian access.

challenges.finding: Compromised continuity of essential (non-Ebola) health services raises indirect mortality.
challenges.response: Effectively implement free care and reinforce continuity of essential health services (CEHS).

challenges.finding: High population mobility raises the risk of rapid geographic expansion; the Wamba/Haut-Uele imported cases stay counted within Nia-Nia (Ituri), and this cycle 2 dead bodies were intercepted at Ituri PoE/PoC.
challenges.response: Reinforce PoE/PoC surveillance and cross-border/inter-provincial coordination.

contacts_total.data_gap_note: Follow-up rate computed only for reporting ZS (completeness 90%); Bunia, Aungba and Nia-Nia (Ituri) had no contact data at consolidation. Sud-Kivu contacts remain exited. No consolidated daily new-contacts / 21-day-completed aggregate was published this cycle (only sub-activity listing). Challenge table cites follow-up at 81.3%.

patient_movement_total.data_gap_note: SitRep51 re-reports the aggregate isolation census 628 at 89.4% occupancy in the banner (unchanged from SitRep50) but published NO fresh patient-movement (CTE/CT/CI) table this cycle. The confirmed/suspected split (215 confirmed / 413 suspected) is carried from SitRep49's last published Tableau 6 split, which still reconciles to the 628 census (215 + 413 = 628); the split is a carried/reconciled estimate, not a freshly reported table. Exit flows sourced from the holistic-care section: 15 recoveries (14 Ituri, 1 Nord-Kivu Katwa) and 8 deaths among previously confirmed CTE patients (7 Ituri, 1 Nord-Kivu Katwa); other 24h movement flows were not published and are shown as 0.

priorities.priority: Resolve the 17 unventilated Ituri confirmed cases without distributing them to named health zones; keep the Wamba/Haut-Uele imported cases counted within Nia-Nia

Relieve isolation load at 89.4% national occupancy and the Nord-Kivu 57.4% CFR; close the reported gap of about 20 isolation centers, accelerate CTE construction/expansion (Mongbwalu +50 beds, HGR Bunia +60 beds, CTE Elikya), and sustain the clinical trial launched at CTE CME Rwampara
Improve contact follow-up (81.6% vs 95% target), reinforce intervention teams in affected ZS, clear the Nord-Kivu laboratory backlog, and protect IPC/EDS teams after the CMC Kiharo attack

preserved_conflicts: SitRep51 headline is DRC-only (1561 confirmed / 506 confirmed deaths across Ituri, Nord-Kivu, Sud-Kivu); the country-scope public snapshot adds the latest reviewed Uganda component separately.

SitRep51 supersedes SitRep50 directly (a single report-day advance), so treat the +33 confirmed and +14 confirmed deaths vs SitRep50 as an official one-day delta for 2026-07-04. Unlike SitRep50, no within-province reclassification was applied this cycle: Ituri is a clean +30 (Rwampara 11, Bunia 7, Nizi 7, Komanda 3, Mongbwalu 2) and Nord-Kivu a clean +3 (Musienene 2, Katwa 1).
SitRep51 carries an explicit Ituri residual of 17 confirmed cases in zones not yet identified; these counts are public but not mappable to named health zones.
No new affected health zone was reported this cycle; the affected-zone count remains 36 of 104 (Ituri 24/36). No new map geometry was required.

The isolation census (628 at 89.4% occupancy) is re-reported only in the SitRep51 banner and is unchanged from SitRep50; SitRep51 published NO fresh patient-movement (CTE/CT/CI) table, so the confirmed/suspected split (215 confirmed / 413 suspected) is carried from SitRep49's last published Tableau 6 split and still reconciles to the 628 census (215 + 413 = 628). It is a carried/reconciled estimate, not a freshly reported table. Nord-Kivu isolation occupancy is cited at 138.8% saturation in the challenge table.
Daily suspected cases (354) and the 110 deceased validated alerts are current-cycle validated alert outputs, not cumulative suspected stock or cumulative confirmed deaths.

Uganda anchor remains 20 cumulative confirmed cases (2 deaths, 11 recoveries) per the Uganda MoH EVD daily dashboard (last reviewed 2026-06-21); WHO AFRO Uganda External SitRep 07 (data as of 2026-06-28) independently reports no new confirmed Uganda cases over the past week. Carried forward this cycle as INSP reports DRC only, so country-scope is 1581 confirmed / 508 deaths / 265 recovered.

Sud-Kivu has recorded no new confirmed case since 2026-05-26, suggesting no recent transmission; it remains at 3 confirmed / 1 death across Miti-Murhesa, and its contacts remain exited from follow-up.
Several low-denominator zones report 100% case-fatality this cycle (Drodro 3/3, Kambala 2/2, Vuhovi 1/1, Lolwa 1/1); these are unstable small-number ratios.

The challenge table cites 96 health-worker infections with 19 deaths (CFR 19.7%), a Nord-Kivu CTE saturation of 138.8%, a Nord-Kivu laboratory backlog amid a 54.2% provincial CFR, an isolation-structure gap of about 20 centers, and a financing gap of about USD 20 million.

A community-death sub-analysis in SitRep51 (94.2% of analysis-cohort deaths occurring in the community, concentrated in Mongbwalu, Bunia and Katwa; late presentation to care) is internally inconsistent with the cumulative confirmed-death total and is treated as qualitative context only, never as a denominator.

External response milestones reported outside the INSP SitRep are tracked as separate context in the LOVS narrative/synthesis, not in the INSP denominators: the WHO Emergency Use Listing of the first BDBV molecular RT-PCR diagnostic (2026-07-02) supports the SitRep-noted decentralization of diagnostic capacity; and the response financing picture (INSP-cited ~USD 20 million pillar-implementation gap; separately, an Africa CDC response requirement of ~USD 1.4 billion with pledges only ~13% released, a White House/OMB supplemental request of ~USD 1.4 billion still before Congress, and OCHA/UNDP macro-humanitarian context) is recorded as distinct facets and not collapsed into a single figure. The clinical trial that began at CTE CME Rwampara on 2026-07-04 is an INSP-reported operational milestone, kept as narrative context and not a denominator.

review_notes: SitRep #051 (report date 2026-07-04; INSP WordPress post 25094 published 2026-07-05T20:26:47Z; embedded PDF media 25095). PDF sha256 b01c0699fb60a6be23703c91b657994b3bdc25dc200f98f561bae3177fedc767, 12 pages, byte-verified against the live INSP upload. Report date 04 July 2026 (publication 05 July 2026).

SitRep51 supersedes SitRep50 directly (single report-day). DRC headline 1561 confirmed / 506 confirmed deaths (CFR 32.4%); +33 confirmed and +14 confirmed deaths vs SitRep50. On 04 July, 33 new confirmed (Ituri 30, Nord-Kivu 3) carried 6 community deaths; a further 8 deaths occurred among previously confirmed CTE patients (7 Ituri, 1 Nord-Kivu Katwa), for +14 total confirmed deaths.

No within-province reclassification this cycle (unlike SitRep50). Ituri is a clean +30 (Rwampara 11, Bunia 7, Nizi 7, Komanda 3, Mongbwalu 2); Nord-Kivu a clean +3 (Musienene 2, Katwa 1). The 1,561 total is still flagged re-actualized and subject to DHIS2 cleaning.
No new affected health zone this cycle. Affected-zone count remains 36 of 104; Ituri stays 24/36 touched. No new map geometry/centroid required.
Health-zone rows reconcile to 1561 confirmed / 506 deaths: 1544 named confirmed cases plus 17 Ituri cases awaiting ventilation, with all 506 confirmed deaths named-zone attributed.

Recovered advanced to 254 DRC cumulative (+15; 14 Ituri, 1 Nord-Kivu). Uganda anchor carried forward at 20 cumulative cases / 2 deaths / 11 recoveries (last reviewed 2026-06-21), so country-scope is 1581 confirmed / 508 deaths / 265 recovered.

Laboratory throughput promoted from section 4.3: Ituri 99 collected/analyzed, 30 positive (30.3%); Nord-Kivu 73 collected/analyzed, 3 positive (4.1%); Sud-Kivu 2 collected and still under analysis (172 analyzed / 33 positive overall, 19.2%).

SitRep51 published NO fresh patient-movement table; the banner re-reports the isolation census 628 at 89.4% (unchanged from SitRep50). The confirmed/suspected split (215/413) is carried from SitRep49 and reconciles to 628. Operational data captured: alerts 1244 of the day / 1002 investigated (80.5%), 354 suspected cases of the day (110 deceased validated alerts), 10079 contacts under follow-up (81.6%, completeness 90%). No consolidated daily new-contacts / 21-day-completed aggregate was published; only sub-activity listing.

Operational milestones this cycle: clinical trials began at CTE CME (Rwampara, Ituri); CTE Beni opened (20 beds); an IPC/EDS team was attacked at CMC Kiharo (Rughenda, Nord-Kivu). External context recorded separately (not in INSP denominators): WHO Emergency Use Listing of the first BDBV molecular diagnostic (2026-07-02); financing facets (INSP ~USD 20M pillar gap; Africa CDC ~USD 1.4B requirement / ~13% released; White House/OMB ~USD 1.4B request pending; OCHA/UNDP macro context) are kept as distinct facets.
